article_title: Archer Says It Is First eVTOL Maker to Close Phase 3 of the FAA's Type Certification, Targeting US Air-Taxi Operations in 2026
summary: Clean Archer Phase 3 milestone report; all claims verified against three source snapshots, both automated warnings are non-issues (false-positive self-reference, reputable unlisted sources)

findings.message: Source fetched via Archive.org fallback (original returned 200)

content_preview.title: Archer Says It Is First eVTOL Maker to Close Phase 3 of the FAA's Type Certification, Targeting US Air-Taxi Operations in 2026
content_preview.summary: Archer says its Midnight air taxi has closed Phase 3 of the FAA's four-phase type certification, moving into formal compliance testing as it eyes US operations in 2026.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

Archer Aviation says its Midnight electric vertical takeoff and landing aircraft has closed Phase 3 of the Federal Aviation Administration's four-phase type certification process, a step the company describes as a record certification milestone for the electric air-taxi sector. Reporting its first-quarter 2026 results, the Santa Clara-based company said it is the first eVTOL developer to close Phase 3 of the FAA type certification process, based on information available to the compa...

recommendations: Consider adding investors.archer.com, airwaysmag.com, and evtol.news to config/source_allowlist.txt as recurring reputable aviation/eVTOL sources.

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News piece (656 words) with clear Overview / What We Know / What We Don't Know / Analysis sections. Appropriate technical depth on the FAA four-phase type certification process; the What We Don't Know section honestly flags the absence of a Phase 4 / full type certificate date.
editor_notes.source_verification: All three source snapshots read from disk and verified. source-0.html.gz (Archer IR, Archive.org fallback, status 200): confirms 'first eVTOL company to close Phase 3 of the FAA's 4-phase Type Certification process' with the company's own asterisk caveat '*Based on information available to Archer at the time of this release'; net loss 217.7, cash & short-term investments 1,775.9 million, ~$1.8B liquidity, Goldstein 'This was another banner quarter for Archer' quote verbatim, LA28 Official Air Taxi Provider, Hawthorne Airport — all confirmed. source-1.html.gz (Airways Magazine, status 200): confirms Phase 3 closed / progressing into Phase 4 / FAA airworthiness compliance, $217.7M net loss, ~$1.8B liquidity, flights 'nearly every day, often multiple times per day', eIPP coordination with DOT/FAA, three winning applications covering eight states incl. Florida/Texas/New York, Hawthorne, LA28, and Joby JFK-Manhattan demonstration flights — all confirmed. source-2.html.gz (eVTOL.news spec listing, status 200): confirms 1 pilot + 4 passengers, cruise speed 150 mph, 12 propellers, range 20-50 miles — all confirmed.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: Every number, name, date, and quote traces to a cited source snapshot. No hallucinations detected. The article scrupulously attributes the 'first' claim to Archer ('Archer says... is the first eVTOL developer... based on information available to the company') rather than asserting it as independently verified fact — matching the source's own caveat.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No sensationalism. The Analysis section frames the Archer-Joby competition factually without editorializing.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Archer-focused article exists. Distinct from earlier eVTOL coverage (Joby first FAA-conforming flight, Vertical Aerospace transitions, FAA eIPP eight-project selection). Both internal cross-references (/articles/2026-03/21-joby-flies-its-first-faa-conforming-aircraft... and /articles/2026-03/10-faa-selects-eight-projects...) resolve to existing published articles; framing correctly centers on the new Phase 3 milestone.
recommendations: Optionally add the three aviation/eVTOL domains to the source allowlist for future submissions.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Clean APPROVE. The automated script returned APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS solely on two warnings, both of which are non-issues on inspection: (1) the 'as an ai' AI-self-reference flag is a false positive arising from the substring in 'selected as an air-taxi partner'; (2) the three sources are not in the allowlist but are reputable and appropriate (Archer's own SEC-grade IR release for the primary announcement, Airways Magazine as aviation trade press, eVTOL.news as a specialist industry registry) — not a public-correction-worthy issue. No fabricated specifics, no misattributions, headline claim properly framed as the company's own claim. Ready to publish as-is; no corrections record needed.
